ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / OKLAHOMA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Oklahoma Pharmacy Technician Registration

Oklahoma Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in Oklahoma run through Oklahoma Board of Pharmacy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Oklahoma by submitting a new application to the Oklahoma Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Oklahoma-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    Meet Eligibility Requirements

    Ensure you are at least 18 years of age (or enrolled in an approved high school program) and possess a high school diploma or GED equivalent.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Create Account and Submit Online Application

    Visit the Oklahoma Board of Pharmacy Online Licensing Portal (ok.gov/pharmacy/Online_Licensing_Portal). Register for a new account, then log in and complete the 'Pharmacy Technician Initial Licensure' application. Pay the non-refundable application fee as part of this step.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Official Transcripts

    Arrange for your official high school transcript or GED certificate to be sent directly to the Oklahoma Board of Pharmacy. Instructions for submission will be provided during the online application process.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete Criminal Background Check

    After submitting your online application, the Board will email instructions for completing a criminal background check through their contracted vendor, IdentoGO. You must follow these instructions to submit your fingerprints and complete the process.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Complete Required Training

    Complete at least 160 hours of training in areas determined essential by the Board, or enroll in a Board-approved pharmacy technician training program, as required by Board Rule 535:10-5-5(f). This training must be completed as specified before licensure.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Await Board Review and Approval

    The Board will review your application, background check results, and all submitted documentation. Once approved and all requirements are met, your pharmacy technician license will be issued.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$100

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Oklahoma Board of Pharmacy
Phone
(405) 521-3815

Application page
Open application

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in Oklahoma follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 1 year
Renewal fee
$60
Continuing education
10 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Oklahoma pharmacy technician license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Oklahoma have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Oklahoma. To work in Oklahoma, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Oklahoma?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Oklahoma while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$100

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 1 year · $60

Licensing board

Oklahoma Board of Pharmacy
